properties.Owner_Name: Eamonn James Percy and Myra Edith Percy (72810)

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
Au - dam does not meet CDA stability standards for rapid drawdown., Boil approx 60cm in diameter noticed on 9JUL21, Hatch issues report 17JUL21. Reservoir level has been lowered, seepage is measured. 

Root cause is excessively hydraulic gradients at the tow of the dam.

2021 DSR provides recommendations. , Dam safety management plan is insufficient to ensure public safety and prevent vandalism, Instrumentation and surveillance are insufficient. , Pn/u - liquefaction assessment has not been completed. IIPs are ongoing, There are no as-builts for this dam. The past 2 DSRs have recommended conducting geotechnical site investigation.

RFP has been released.

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
Due to the age of the culvert, it should be scheduled for replacement within the next 5 years; or sooner if spillway replacement is completed., Failure to replace the spillway, would likely lead to a partial failure which would lead to progressive collapse of the
dam, resulting in flooding as noted in the Ecora report titled Re-determination of Consequences Classification
dated October 2018. Although it is a low risk level for human injury or death, the dam does have a high probably of failure.

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
The McCuddy Creek Diversion is deficient and poses a risk of overtopping to this dam., The dam does not meet CDA minimum safety factors under FSL loading.

Recommendation from DSR:
Complete remedial work to increase the safety factor (i.e., extend support berm, flatten d/s slope, etcetera)., The freeboard is less than the minimum recommended value of 1.0 m and the crest elevation is lower at the centre of the dam.

Recommendation from DSR:
Regrade and level the dam to a minimum elevation of 1048.4 m (+0.3 m at the lowest point of the dam and 1.0 m above spillway invert)., The spillway requires maintenance and the conveyance and terminal section of the spillway does not have adequate erosion protection.

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description: An - Seismic withstand capacity assessment needs to be completed, particularly gate hoist structure and spillway., An - dedicated spillway backup power, Concrete condition is fair to poor in many places. Active leakage in spillway cracks and exposed reinforcing bar, Post Tensioned Anchors – protective caps are in poor condition and need to be addressed. Load verification needs to be performed, Public Safety Management Plan is outdated, signage does not meet CDA guidelines and navigation safety boom should be considered

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
Approx. 10-20m wide section near the Right Abutment that is 1.0m lower than the crest of the dam. This was determined after a drone survey before the 2021 DSR.

Issues:
- mechanism for this depression not known
- the dam does not have sufficient freeboard to pass the IDF.


 , Collect readings from instrumentation and analyze and interpret the readings

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
Brushing of vegetation and access to seepage weirs must improve., CoR does not collect readings from instrumentation and analyze and interpret the readings, specifically reservoir elevation and seepage weir flowrates.	, No documentation of site surveillance, No formal Consequence Classification Assessment

This is mentioned in the 2021 Annual Inspection Report. In addition, the dam owner must complete this annually., No records that mechanical components are tested

The lower-level outlet valve is in the open position. There is no indication that this valve can be closed or that it is tested., The 2018 DSR was not completed, this is in progress., The DEP manual is not finalized, The OMS manual is not finalized

properties.Owner_Name: Peachland, The Corporation of the District of (34289)

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
The 2020 inundation and consequence classification review by KWL recommended increasing the consequence classification from the previous designation of High to Very High., The 2022 Dam Safety Review prepared by KWL recommended the dam consequence classification be Very High. After review of previous correspondence, it was noted that Rob McLean accepted the consequence classification of Very High on March 4, 2020, via email to the dam owner., The existing spillway is significantly undersized and needs to be replaced to safely convey the IDF.

Recommendation from DSR:
Proceed with the design of the new spillway and construct as soon as practical (recommended
within 2 years).

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description: An - deterioration of steel and concrete components. Refer to 2021 DSR. , Au - Backup diesel power is not directly connected to the gate hoist and cannot independently operate the spillway in an emergency, Au - spillway does not have sufficient capacity to pass IDF. (8025cms vs 8600cms). Study conducted in 1999.

properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency, Regulatory Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description: An - #4 - Test mechanical components of dam - several items have no record of testing., An - #5 Collect and Interpret Readings - instrument data is not being reviewed or assessed on a regular basis, An - The upstream lower LLO is blocked., An - There is no spillway, the dam cannot safely pass the IDF. The "Internal Spillway" does not meet CDA principles since it feeds into two 600mm steel pipes, An - downstream slope does not meet CDA stability criteria for static and pseudo static loading conditions, An - formal inspection frequency is not consistent with the B.C. Dam Safety Regulation, An - there is not an effective means to draw down the reservoir in an emergency, An - upstream slope does not meet CDA FoS criteria for drawdown of 5cms as stated in the OMS, Pn - The Rose Valley Dam is susceptible to several potential internal erosion mechanisms., Pn - overflow spill conduit and intake tower are deteriorating, assessment required, Pn - reservoir slope stability unknown, Pn - upstream and downstream concrete conduits have a low steel/concrete ratio and have never been structurally assessed, Pu - potentially susceptible to liquefaction, and the on-file post-seismic strength analysis is deficient.

properties.Spillway_Name: East Channel Overflow Dam, East Side Overflow Dam
properties.Deficiency_Type: Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency, Physical Deficiency
properties.Deficiency_Description:
An - Concrete condition is fair to poor in many places on the overflow structures. 2018 Concrete Condition Assessment identified 19 areas needing 18.1M dollars worth of work to be completed within 5 years., An - Existing Instrumentation insufficient to monitor dam. IIP was provided to FortisBC on January 24, 2022. Stantec report advised installing 14 piezos, 4 tiltmeters, 3x dataloggers and seismic equipment, An - Seismic withstand capacity of spillway has not
been assessed , An - The existing signage, especially upstream warning signage, is out of date and does not adequately warn of the potential hazard that the overflow dams create. This signage should be re-assessed, especially considering the proximity of a publicly accessible beach immediately upstream of the East Overflow dam.

Determine whether a navigation safety boom upstream of the South Slocan Dam overflow
dams is appropriate, An - The recommended consequence rating for a sunny day scenario is “Very High”. Based on this, FortisBC should proceed with plans to assess the seismic withstand capacity of the dam for the associated design.
